job_description.title: Senior AI/ML Engineer
job_description.description: Redrob AI is looking for a Senior AI/ML Engineer to design, develop, and deploy advanced AI models for talent acquisition. The role involves working with large-scale datasets, implementing semantic search algorithms, and applying state-of-the-art NLP techniques and LLMs. Requirements: 5+ years of experience in AI/ML, proficiency in Python, TensorFlow/PyTorch, experience with NLP and vector embeddings.
requirements: 5+ years of experience in AI/ML development
Proficiency in Python and relevant frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch)
Solid understanding of vector embeddings and similarity search

candidates.name: Alice Smith
candidates.resume_text: Alice Smith is a highly experienced AI/ML Engineer with 6 years of experience in developing and deploying machine learning models. She has a strong background in NLP and has worked with various LLMs. She is proficient in Python, PyTorch, and has extensive experience with vector databases and semantic search.

candidates.name: Bob Johnson
candidates.resume_text: Bob Johnson is a Software Engineer with 4 years of experience in full-stack development. He has some experience with Python and has worked on basic data analysis projects. He is interested in transitioning into AI/ML and has completed several online courses in machine learning.

candidates.name: Charlie Brown
candidates.resume_text: Charlie Brown is a Senior Data Scientist with 7 years of experience in building predictive models and analyzing large datasets. He is proficient in Python, R, and has worked with various machine learning libraries. He has some experience with NLP and is interested in applying his skills to talent acquisition.

candidates.name: Diana Prince
candidates.resume_text: Diana Prince is an AI Researcher with a PhD in Computer Science and 3 years of experience in developing state-of-the-art NLP models. She has published several papers in top-tier conferences and is highly proficient in Python and TensorFlow. Her research focuses on LLMs and semantic understanding.
